title: Sourcetype Breakdown Trending
spl:
index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=license_usage
| stats sum(b) as bytes by st
| eval gb=round(bytes/1024/1024/1024,2)
| sort -gb
| head 20
description: Understanding data volume per sourcetype enables cost optimization, retention tuning, and unexpected growth detection.
value: Understanding data volume per sourcetype enables cost optimization, retention tuning, and unexpected growth detection.
implementation: Track daily volume per sourcetype. Identify top consumers. Alert on sourcetypes with unexpected growth (>20% week-over-week). Use for license optimization and retention policy tuning.
visualization: Bar chart (top sourcetypes by volume), Pie chart (volume distribution), Line chart (growth trend for top sourcetypes).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Monitoring Console.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `_internal` (license_usage.log).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Track daily volume per sourcetype. Identify top consumers. Alert on sourcetypes with unexpected growth (>20% week-over-week). Use for license optimization and retention policy tuning.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=license_usage
| stats sum(b) as bytes by st
| eval gb=round(bytes/1024/1024/1024,2)
| sort -gb
| head 20
```

Understanding this SPL

**Sourcetype Breakdown Trending** ‚Äî Understanding data volume per sourcetype enables cost optimization, retention tuning, and unexpected growth detection.

Documented **Data sources**: `_internal` (license_usage.log). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Monitoring Console. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _internal; **sourcetype**: splunkd. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=_internal, sourcetype=splunkd. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by st** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **gb** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
‚Ä¢ Limits the number of rows with `head`.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (top sourcetypes by volume), Pie chart (volume distribution), Line chart (growth trend for top sourcetypes).
grandmaExplanation: Understanding data volume per log type enables cost optimization, retention tuning, and unexpected growth detection ‚Äî so you always trust the numbers you are looking at.
